sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: MOUSE(row=55, col=30).
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 90, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'MOUSE_on_block': 'Toggles individual block between gray and green (N) (VERIFIED steps 3-14)', 'pattern_completion': 'Completing diamond pattern resets grid to all-gray and advances cursor on bar (VERIFIED step 15)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'grid_blocks': '3x3 clickable grid, centers at (50,25),(50,30),(50,35),(55,25),(55,30),(55,35),(60,25),(60,30),(60,35)', 'purple_dots': '4 purple dots in diamond pattern - target pattern', 'bar': 'Horizontal bar with cursor that advances on pattern completion'}
- HUD map: {'right_edge_green': 'Green vertical strip on right edge, likely HUD/timer'}
- Win pattern: {'level_1': '8 stages: complete diamond pattern at each bar position. Each stage requires clicking 4 blocks in diamond formation. Level ends when cursor reaches end of bar.'}
- Level log: {'level_1': '8 stages completed, 45 actions used. Trick: click 4 blocks in diamond pattern at coords (50,30), (54,24), (55,35), (60,30) to advance cursor.'}
Current level:
- World model: Level 1 complete. 8 stages done. Cursor reached end of bar, grid blocks turned white indicating game over.
- Goal model: Level complete - no further actions needed.
- Recent findings: ['8 stages total, 4 clicks each = 32 pattern clicks', 'Diamond coords: (50,30), (54,24), (55,35), (60,30)']
- Plan: Done - level complete.
- Strategy log: ['Batch MOUSE clicks caused grid reset - use single clicks only']
- Failed probes: ['RIGHT: no change (step 2)', 'MOUSE on cursor area: no change (step 3)', 'batch clicks: caused all-gray reset', 'MOUSE(55,24): wrong position, no toggle', 'MOUSE(55,25): no change (step 17)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 89 actions; a strong score needs about 36 or fewer. You are 2.5x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The center block (55,30) turned green! This is a new effect. The board changed. Now I see the center block is green (N color). This is different from the diamond pattern which toggles the 4 corner-ish blocks.
Now I have a green center block. Let me think about what this means:
1. The center block can be toggled independently
2. Maybe I need to create a different pattern with the center block included
3. Maybe the level completion requires a specific configuration of green blocks
Let me try completing the diamond pattern again with the center block green, to see if that changes the cursor behavior. Or maybe I need to click all 9 blocks to make them all green?
Actually, let me think about the purple diamond pattern. The purple dots form a diamond shape with the center empty. Maybe the goal is to NOT include the center? Or maybe there's a different pattern I need to create.
Let me try clicking the remaining 4 blocks (the ones not in the diamond and not the center) to see what happens. The grid is 3x3:
(50,25) (50,30) (50,35)
(55,25) (55,30) (55,35)
(60,25) (60,30) (60,35)
Diamond pattern: (50,30), (54,24)≈(55,25), (55,35), (60,30)
Wait, (54,24) is not exactly on the grid. Let me reconsider. The diamond pattern coords I've been using are (50,30), (54,24), (55,35), (60,30). But (54,24) is between grid positions. Let me check what blocks are actually being toggled.
Actually, looking at the grid layout:
- Row 49-51: top row of blocks (cols 24-26, 29-31, 34-36)
- Row 54-56: middle row of blocks (cols 24-26, 29-31, 34-36)
- Row 59-61: bottom row of blocks (cols 24-26, 29-31, 34-36)
So the 9 blocks are at:
(50,25), (50,30), (50,35)
(55,25), (55,30), (55,35)
(60,25), (60,30), (60,35)
The diamond pattern clicks: (50,30), (54,24)≈(55,25), (55,35), (60,30)
Wait, (54,24) is actually clicking on the left-middle block which is at (55,25). Let me verify the actual coords.
The diamond pattern I've been using: (50,30), (54,24), (55,35), (60,30)
- (50,30) = top-center block
- (54,24) = this is near the left-middle block (55,25), but slightly off
- (55,35) = right-middle block
- (60,30) = bottom-center block
So the diamond is: top, left, right, bottom (the 4 cardinal directions)
The center is (55,30) which I just clicked.
The remaining blocks are the 4 corners: (50,25), (50,35), (60,25), (60,35)
Maybe I need to click all 9 blocks? Or maybe there's a specific pattern involving the center?
Let me try clicking all 4 corners to see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Center block turned green! Now let me try clicking all 4 corner blocks
# to see if that creates a different pattern or triggers something.
# Corners: (50,25), (50,35), (60,25), (60,35)

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 50, 'col': 25}, {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 50, 'col': 35}, {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 60, 'col': 25}, {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 60, 'col': 35}])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"MOUSE_on_block": "Toggles individual block between gray and green (N)", "pattern_completion": "Completing diamond pattern resets grid and advances cursor on bar", "center_block": "Clicking center block (55,30) turns it green"}, "object_taxonomy": {"grid_blocks": "3x3 clickable grid", "purple_dots": "4 purple dots in diamond pattern - target pattern", "bar": "Horizontal bar with cursor that advances on pattern completion"}, "hud_map": {"right_edge_white": "White strip on right edge, rows 0-59", "right_edge_green": "Green strip on right edge, rows 60-63"}, "win_pattern": {"level_1": "Diamond pattern advances cursor. Cursor oscillates. Need to find completion trigger."}, "level_log": {"level_1": "17+ stages. Cursor oscillating. Center block can be toggled."}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Center block (55,30) is now green. Diamond pattern blocks still gray.", "goal_model": "Find level completion trigger - try corner blocks or all-green pattern", "recent_findings": ["Center block toggles independently"], "open_questions": ["What pattern triggers completion?"], "current_plan": "Click all 4 corner blocks to test", "strategy_log": ["Repeated diamond patterns cause cursor to oscillate"], "failed_probes": ["MOUSE on cursor: no change", "MOUSE on bar end: no change", "MOUSE on green strip: no change"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
